author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XX. 29, 30.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And as they departed from Jericho, great multitudes followed Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, behold, two blind men sitting by the wayside, when they heard that Jesus passed by, cried out, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, Thou Son of David.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See whence He passed unto Jerusalem, and where He abode before this, with regard to which it seems to me especially worthy of inquiry, wherefore He went not away even long before this from thence unto Galilee, but through Samaria.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this we will leave to them that are fond of learning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if any one were disposed to search the matter out carefully, he will find that John intimates it well, and hath expressed the cause.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us keep to the things set before us, and let us listen to these blind men, who were better than many that see.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither having a guide, nor being able to see Him when come near to them, nevertheless they strove to come unto Him, and began to cry with a loud voice, and when rebuked for speaking, they cried the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such is the nature of an enduring soul, by the very things that hinder, it is borne up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Christ suffered them to be rebuked, that their earnestness might the more appear, and that thou mightest learn that worthily they enjoy the benefits of their cure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He doth not so much as ask, “Do ye believe?” as He doth with many; for their cry, and their coming unto Him, sufficed to make their faith manifest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hence learn, O beloved, that though we be very vile and outcast, but yet approach God with earnestness, even by ourselves we shall be able to effect whatsoever we ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See, for instance, these men, how, having none of the apostles to plead with them, but rather many to stop their mouths, they were able to pass over the hindrances, and to come unto Jesus Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet the evangelist bears witness to no confidence of lifein them, but earnestness sufficed them instead of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These then let us also emulate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though God defer the gift, though there be many withdrawing us, let us not desist from asking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in this way most of all shall we win God to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See at least even here, how not poverty, not blindness, not their being unheard, not their being rebuked by the multitude, not anything else, impeded their exceeding earnestness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such is the nature of a fervent and toiling soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He called them, and said, What will ye that I should do unto you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They say unto Him, Lord, that our eyes may be opened.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore doth He ask them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Lest any one should think that when they wish to receive one thing, He giveth them another thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed it is usual with Him on every occasion, first to make manifest and discover to all the virtue of those He is healing, and then to apply the cure, for one reason, that He might lead on the others likewise to emulation; and for another, that He might show that they were enjoying the gift worthily.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This, for instance, He did with respect to the Canaanitish woman also, this too in the case of the centurion, this again as to her that had the issue of blood, or rather that marvellous woman even anticipated the Lord’s inquiry; but not so did He pass her by, but even after the cure makes her manifest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such earnest care had He on every occasion to proclaim the good deeds of them that come to Him, and to show them to be much greater than they are,which He doth here also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, when they said what they wished, He had compassion on them, and touched them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this alone is the cause of their cure, for which also He came into the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But nevertheless, although it be mercy and grace, it seeks for the worthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But that they were worthy is manifest, both from what they cried out, and from the fact that, when they had received, they did not hasten away, as many do, being ungrateful after the benefits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, they were not like this, but were both persevering before the gift, and after the gift grateful, for “they followed Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when He drew nigh unto Jerusalem, and was come to Bethphage, unto the Mount of Olives, He sent two of His disciples, saying, Go into the village over against you, and ye shall find an ass tied, and a colt with her: loose them, and bring them unto me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if any man say aught unto you, ye shall say, The Lord hath need of them; and straightway he sendeth them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Zechariah the prophet, Tell ye the daughter of Sion, Behold, thy King cometh to thee, meek, and sitting upon an ass, and a colt the foal of an ass.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet He had often entered Jerusalem before, but never with so much circumstance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the cause?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was the beginning then of the dispensation; and neither was He very well known, nor the time of His passion near; wherefore He mixed with them with less distinction, and more disguising Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He would not have been held in admiration, had He so appeared, and He would have excited them to greater anger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when He had both given them sufficient proof of His power, and the cross was at the doors, He makes Himself then more conspicuous, and doeth with greater circumstance all the things that were likely to inflame them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was indeed possible for this to have been done at the beginning also; but it was not profitable nor expedient it should be so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou observe, I pray thee, how many miracles are done, and how many prophecies are fulfilled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said, “Ye shall find an ass;” He foretold that no man should hinder them, but that all, when they heard, should hold their peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this is no small condemnation of the Jews, if them that were never known to Him, neither had appeared before Him, He persuades to give up their own property, and to say nothing against it, and that by His disciples, while these, being present with Him at the working of His miracles, were not persuaded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And do not account what was done to be a small thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who persuaded them, when their own property was taken from them, and that, when they were perhaps poor men and husbandmen, not to forbid it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why say I not to forbid it? not to ask, or even if they asked, to hold their peace, and give it up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed both things were alike marvellous, as well, if they said nothing, when their beasts were dragged away, or if having spoken, and heard, “The Lord hath need of them,” they yielded and withstood not, and this when they see not Him, but His disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By these things He teaches them, that it was in His power to have entirely hindered the Jews also, even against their will, when they were proceeding to attack Him, and to have made them speechless, but He would not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And another thing again together with these doth He teach the disciples, to give whatever He should ask; and, though he should require them to yield up their very life, to give even this, and not to gainsay.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if even strangers gave up to Him, much more ought they to strip themselves of all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides what we have said, He was fulfilling also another prophecy, one which was twofold, one part in words, and another in deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that in deeds was, by the sitting on the ass; and that by words, the prediction of Zacharias; because he had said, that the King should sit on an ass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He, having sat and having fulfilled it, gave to the prophecy another beginning again, by what He was doing typifying beforehand the things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How and in what manner?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He proclaimed beforehand the calling of the unclean Gentiles, and that He should rest upon them, and that they should yield to Him and follow Him, and prophecy succeeded to prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But to me He seemeth not for this object only to sit on the ass, but also as affording us a standard of self-denial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not only did He fulfill prophecies, nor did He only plant the doctrines of the truth, but by these very things He was correcting our practice for us, everywhere setting us rules of necessary use, and by all means amending our life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause, I say, even when He was to be born He sought not a splendid house, nor a mother rich and distinguished, but a poor woman, and one that had a carpenter as her betrothed husband; and is born in a shed, and laid in a manger: and choosing His disciples, He chose not orators and wise men, not rich men and nobly born, but poor men, and of poor families, and in every way undistinguished; and providing His table, at one time He sets before Himself barley loaves, and at another at the very moment commands the disciples to buy at the market.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And making His couch, He makes it of grass, and putting on raiment, He clothes Himself in what is cheap, and in no respect different from the common sort; and a house He did not so much as possess.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if He had to go from place to place, He did this travelling on foot, and so travelling, as even to grow weary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And sitting, He requires no throne nor pillow, but sits on the ground, sometimes in the mountain, and sometimes by the well, and not merely by the well, but also alone, and talks with a Samaritan woman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, setting measures of sorrow, when He had need to mourn, He weeps moderately, everywhere setting us rules, as I have said, and limits how far one ought to proceed, and not any further.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So for this intent now also, since it happens that some are weak and have need of beasts to carry them, in this too He fixes a measure, showing that one ought not to yoke horses or mules to be borne by them, but to use an ass, and not to proceed further, and everywhere to be limited by the want.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us look also at the prophecy, that by words, that by acts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the prophecy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Behold, thy King cometh to thee, meek, and riding on an ass, and a young colt;”not driving chariots, like the rest of the kings, not demanding tributes, not thrusting men off, and leading about guards, but displaying His great meekness even hereby.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ask then the Jew, what King came to Jerusalem borne on an ass?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, he could not mention, but this alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He did these things, as I said, signifying beforehand the things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For here the church is signified by the colt, and the new people, which was once unclean, but which, after Jesus sat on them, became clean.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see the image preserved throughout.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean that the disciples loose the asses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For by the apostles, both they and we were called; by the apostles were we brought near.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But because our acceptance provoked them also to emulation, therefore the ass appears following the colt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after Christ hath sat on the Gentiles, then shall they also come moving us to emulation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Paul declaring this, said, “That blindnessin part is happened to Israel, until the fullness of the Gentiles be come in; and so all Israel shall be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that it was a prophecy is evident from what is said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither would the prophet have cared to express with such great exactness the age of the ass, unless this had been so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not these things only are signified by what is said, but also that the apostles should bring them with ease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as here, no man gainsaid them so as to keep the asses, so neither with regard to the Gentiles was any one able to prevent them, of those who were before masters of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He doth not sit on the bare colt, but on the apostles’ garments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after they had taken the colt, they then gave up all, even as Paul also said, “I will very gladly spend and be spent for your souls.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark how tractable the colt, how being unbroken, and having never known the rein, he was not restive, but went on orderly; which thing itself was a prophecy of the future, signifying the submissiveness of the Gentiles, and their sudden conversion to good order.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all things did that word work, which said, “Loose him, and bring him to me:” so that the unmanageable became orderly, and the unclean thenceforth clean.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But see the baseness of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He had wrought so many miracles, and never were they thus amazed at Him; but when they saw a multitude running together, then they marvel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For all the city was moved, saying, Who is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the multitudes said, This is Jesus the prophet of Nazareth of Galilee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when they thought they were saying something great, even then were their thoughts earthly, and low, and dragging on the ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things He did, not as displaying any pomp, but at once, as I have said, both fulfilling a prophecy, and teaching self-denial, and at the same time also comforting His disciples, who were grieving for His death, and showing them that He suffers all these things willingly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark thou, I pray thee, the accuracy of the prophet, how he foretold all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And some things David, some things Zechariah, had proclaimed beforehand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us also do likewise, and let us sing hymns, and give up our garments to them that bear Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what should we deserve, when some clothe the ass on which He was set, and others strew the garments even under her feet; but we, seeing him naked, and not being even commanded to strip ourselves, but to spend of what is laid by, not even so are liberal?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when they indeed attend upon Him before and behind, but we, when He cometh unto us, send Him away, and thrust Him off and insult Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How sore a punishment do these things deserve, how great vengeance!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thy Lord cometh unto thee in need, and thou art not willing so much as to listen to His entreaty, but thou blamest and rebukest Him, and this, when thou hast heard such words as these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if in giving one loaf, and a little money, thou art so mean, and haughty, and backward; if thou hadst to empty out all, what wouldest thou become?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou not those that show their magnificence in the theatre, how much they give away to the harlots? but thou givest not so much as the half, nay often not the smallest part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the devil is exhorting to give to whom it may chance, procuring us hell, and thou givest; but Christ to the needy, promising a kingdom, and thou, far from giving, dost rather insult them, and thou choosest rather to obey the devil, that thou mightest be punished, than to submit to Christ, and be saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what could be worse than this frenzy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: One procures hell, the other a kingdom, and ye leave the latter, and run unto the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this ye send away, when He cometh unto you, that when he is far off, ye call unto you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what you do is the same as if a king bearing a royal robe, and offering a diadem, did not win your choice, but a robber brandishing a sword at you, and threatening death, were to win it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Considering these things then, beloved, let us discern the truth at length though late, and let us grow sober.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I am now ashamed of speaking of almsgiving, because that having often spoken on this subject, I have effected nothing worth the exhortation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For some increase indeed hath there been, but not so much as I wished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I see you sowing, but not with a liberal hand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore I fear too lest ye also “reap sparingly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that we do sow sparingly, let us inquire, if it seem good, whichare more numerous in the city, poor or rich; and which they, who are neitherpoor nor rich, but have a middle place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As, for instance, a tenth part is of rich, and a tenth of the poor that have nothing at all, and the rest of the middle sort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us distribute then amongst the poor the whole multitude of the city, and ye will see the disgrace how great it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the very rich indeed are but few, but those that come next to them are many; again, the poor are much fewer than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless, although there are so many that are able to feed the hungry, many go to sleep in their hunger, not because those that have are not able with ease to succor them, but because of their great barbarity and inhumanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if both the wealthy, and those next to them, were to distribute amongst themselves those who are in need of bread and raiment, scarcely would one poor person fall to the share of fifty men or even a hundred.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet nevertheless, though in such great abundance of persons to assist them, they are wailing every day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that thou mayest learn the inhumanity of the others, when the church is possessed of a revenue of one of the lowest among the wealthy, and not of the very rich, consider how many widows it succors every day, how many virgins; for indeed the list of them hath already reached unto the number of three thousand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Together with these, she succors them that dwell in the prison, the sick in the caravansera, the healthy, those that are absent from their home, those that are maimed in their bodies, those that wait upon the altar; and with respect to food and raiment, them that casually come every day; and her substance is in no respect diminished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that if ten men only were thus willing to spend, there would be no poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what, it will be said, are our children to inherit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The principal remains, and the income again is become more abundant, the goods being stored up for them in Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But are ye not willing to do this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At least do it by the half, at least by the third part, at least by the fourth part, at least by the tenth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For owing to God’s favor, it were possible for our city to nourish the poor of ten cities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if ye will, let us make some calculationin proof of this; or rather there is no need so much as of reckoning; for of itself the easiness of the thing is discernible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See at least, upon public occasions, how much one house hath often not been backward to spend, and hath not had so much as a little feeling of the expense, which service if each of the rich were willing to perform for the poor, in a brief moment of time he would have seized on Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What plea then will there be? what shadow of defense, when not even of the things from which we must assuredly be separated, when taken away from hence, not even of these do we impart to the needy with as much liberality as others to those on the stage, and this when we are to reap so many benefits therefrom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we ought indeed, even though we were always to be here, not even so to be sparing of this good expenditure; but when after a little time, we are to be removed from hence, and dragged away naked from all, what kind of defense shall we have for not even out of our income giving to the hungry and distressed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither do I constrain thee to lessen thy possessions, not because I do not wish it, but because I see thee very backward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not then this I say, but spend of your fruits, and treasure up nothing from these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is enough for thee to have the money of thine income pouring in on thee as from a fountain; make the poor sharers with thee, and become a good steward of the things given thee of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But I pay tribute, one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause then dost thou despise, because in this case no one demands it of thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the other, who, should the earth bear, or should it not bear, takes by force, and extorts, thou darest not gainsay; but Him that is so mild, and then only demands, when the earth bears, thou answerest not even to a word?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And who will deliver thee from those intolerable punishments?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is no one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if, because in the other case a very sore punishment will ensue to thee for not giving, therefore thou becomest diligent about the payment, consider here too is one more sore; not to be bound, neither to be cast into prison, but to depart into the eternal fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all reasons then let us pay these tributes first: for great is the facility, and greater the reward; and more abundant the gain, and worse the punishments to us if we are obstinate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For a punishment cometh upon us, which hath no end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou tell me of the soldier’s fighting for thee with the barbarians, there is here too a camp, that of the poor, and a war, which the poor are waging for thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when they receive, by praying they make God propitious; and making Him propitious, they repulse, instead of barbarians, the assaults of the devils; they suffer not the evil one to be violent, neither to attack us continually, but they relax his might.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seeing therefore these soldiers every day fighting in thy behalf with the devil by their supplications and prayers, demand of thyself this good contribution, their nourishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this King being mild hath not assigned thee any to demand it of thee, but desires thou shouldest give it willingly; though thou pay by little and little, He receives it; though being in difficulty, thou shouldest pay after a long time, He doth not press him that hath not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then despise His long-suffering; let us treasure up for ourselves, not wrath, but salvation; not death, but life; not punishment and vengeance, but honors and crowns.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is no need in this case to pay a hire for the conveyance of the things contributed; there is no need in this case to labor in turning them into money.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou givest them up, the Lord Himself removes them into Heaven; He Himself makes the traffic the more gainful for thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is no need here to find one to carry in what thou hast contributed; contribute only, and straightway it goeth up, not that others may be maintained as soldiers, but that it may remain for thee with great profit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For herewhatsoever thou mayest have given, it is not possible to recover; but there thou wilt receive them again with much honor, and shalt gain greater, and more spiritual gains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here the gifts are a demand; there a loan, and money at interest, and a debt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea farther, God hath given thee bonds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “he that showeth mercy to a poor man,” it is said, “lendeth to the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He gave thee also an earnest, and bail, and this being God!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What sort of earnest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The things in the present life, the visible, the spiritual things, the foretaste of the things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then dost thou delay, and why art thou backward, having received so many things already, looking for so many things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what thou hast received are these: He Himself made thee a body, He Himself put in thee a soul, He honored with speech thee alone of the things on the earth, He gave thee the use of all the things that are seen, He bestowed on thee the knowledge of Himself, He gave up His Son for thee, He gave thee a baptism full of so many good things, He gave thee a holy table, He promised a kingdom, and the good things that cannot be told.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having then received so many good things, having to receive so many, again I say the same thing, art thou making petty reckoning about perishing riches, and what excuse wilt thou have?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But art thou looking altogether at thy children? and dost draw back for the sake of these?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, rather teach them also to gain such gains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou hadst money lent out and bearing interest, and thou hadst a grateful debtor, thou wouldest ten thousand times rather choose instead of the gold to leave the bond to thy child, so that he should have the large income from it, and not be constrained to go about, and seek for others to borrow it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And now give this bond to thy children, and leave God a debtor to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou dost not sell thy lands, and give to thy children, but leavest them, that the income may remain, and that they may have a greater increase of riches from thence; but this bond, which is more productive than any land or revenue, and bears so many fruits, this art thou afraid to leave to them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What great folly must this be, and frenzy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this when thou knowest, that though thou shouldest leave it to them, thou thyself also shall again take it away with thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of this nature are the things spiritual; they have great munificence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then be beggarly; neither be inhuman and savage towards ourselves, but let us traffic in that good merchandise; that we may both ourselves take it away with us when we depart, and leave it to our own children, and attain to the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom be unto the Father, together with the Holy Ghost, glory, might, honor, now and ever, and world without end.
